title: The Impact of Large Language Models on Task Automation in Manufacturing Services

agents.description: Text correction agent that uses GPT-3.5-turbo to automatically correct spelling and grammar errors in reply emails for technical service incidents.
Summarization agent that uses GPT-4 to generate concise summaries (100, 200, or 500 words) of customer request and solution message exchanges for technical service incidents.

Question answering agent using RAG architecture. Retrieves relevant historical incident records from a Chroma vector database and uses GPT-3.5-turbo to generate context-aware answers to synthetic customer inquiries.

orchestration.orchestration_pattern_description: Three separate single-step pipelines are implemented for three distinct cognitive tasks: text correction, summarization, and question answering. Each pipeline operates independently. The question answering pipeline uses a RAG-based single-step orchestration where a vector search retrieves relevant chunks from Chroma DB and the result is inserted into a prompt template before being sent to the LLM.

human_integration.human_integration_description: Human technical service agents initiate queries and customer requests. Manual validation is performed by researchers comparing LLM outputs against human-generated solutions. The system is designed to assist human support agents by automating text correction, summarization, and question answering, freeing them to focus on complex tasks.

retrieval.application_domain_description: Technical service and support for production machinery manufacturers, including handling customer inquiries about machine configuration, troubleshooting outages, installation procedures, and maintenance activities.
retrieval.problem_description: Manual activities in manufacturing technical service—such as correcting text in reply emails, summarizing customer communication exchanges, and answering recurring customer queries—are time-consuming and knowledge-intensive. It remains unclear which of these cognitive tasks can be fully automated using LLMs, and there is insufficient research validating LLM automation potential with real customer data in manufacturing service contexts.
retrieval.proposed_solution: A prototyping approach using real customer incident data to validate LLM-based automation of three cognitive tasks: (1) text correction using GPT-3.5-turbo, (2) text summarization using GPT-4, and (3) question answering using a RAG architecture with Chroma DB vector database, OpenAI text-embedding-3-small embeddings, LangChain framework, and GPT-3.5-turbo for response generation.
retrieval.research_methodology_description: Prototyping methodology using 15 real customer technical service requests. Three prototypes are built and validated: text correction (typos introduced and corrected), summarization (cosine similarity and manual analysis of summaries at 100/200/500 words), and question answering (10 synthetic queries per incident, vector search relevance, and cosine distance analysis of LLM responses against historical records).

decision_quality_improvement: Text correction: Almost all typos were eliminated by the automated process without changing wording or message content.
Summarization: Cosine similarities between summaries and source texts ranged from 0.63 to 0.86, increasing with longer summaries.
Question answering: Vector search returned 100% relevant chunks when retrieving 1 chunk, 95% for 2 chunks, and 87% for 3 chunks.
Question answering: Mean cosine distance of LLM responses to relevant original request chunks was 0.5, versus 0.9 for nearest irrelevant chunks.
response_time_impact: Summarization saves substantial processing time for customer service agents; time saved per incident ranged from approximately 0.38 to 11.68 minutes depending on summary length and incident complexity.
Automation of question answering minimizes time agents spend searching for solutions in large datasets.
reliability_metrics: GPT-4 produced summaries with average word count of 100.33 words at 100-word specification.
GPT-4 produced significantly shorter text than specified at 500-word specification (average: 268.4 words).
GPT-3.5-turbo showed significant text length deviations in summarization compared to GPT-4.

ce_technique_effectiveness.retrieval-augmented-generation: RAG with vector search achieved high relevance of retrieved chunks (100% for k=1, 95% for k=2, 87% for k=3) and LLM responses showed low cosine distance to relevant historical records (mean 0.5), demonstrating effective grounding of answers in historical data.
ce_technique_effectiveness.in-context-learning: Providing context via prompt template improved accuracy and relevance of question answering responses.

failure_modes: Ambiguously formulated customer requests led to higher cosine distance to relevant chunks and lower separation from irrelevant chunks (e.g., Inc4).
GPT-4 failed to adhere to specified word count for longer summaries (500-word target produced average of 268.4 words).
Knowledge hallucination identified as a challenge for LLMs in manufacturing service contexts.
Reliability of LLM-generated output remains a challenge for fully automated systems.
limitations: Small sample size of 15 customer requests may not fully capture variability of real-world scenarios.
Dataset limited to IT-related topics (software, hardware, network), not covering all manufacturing machine operation issues.
Challenges with ambiguous inputs, knowledge hallucination, and seamless integration with human workflows remain unresolved.
Scalability of solutions in diverse manufacturing environments not yet evaluated.
Long-term impact on operational performance not assessed.

baseline_methods: Human technical support agents (manual text correction, summarization, and question answering by human experts)
gpt-3.5-turbo-0125 compared against gpt-4-0125-preview for summarization text length accuracy
identified_research_gaps: Scalability of LLM solutions in diverse manufacturing environments
Improving prompt engineering for ambiguous inputs
Refining LLM training with domain-specific data
Ensuring seamless integration with human workflows
Addressing limitations from small sample size used in prototyping
Handling ambiguous inputs in question answering tasks
